supply_chain.dependencies: Largest suppliers are Pentair plc (20% of cost of products sold), Zodiac Pool Systems, Inc. (12%), and Hayward Pool Products, Inc. (11%). Decision by largest suppliers to sell directly to retailers bypassing distributors would adversely affect business.
supply_chain.risks: Failure to maintain favorable relationships with suppliers could adversely affect business. Supplier difficulties or disruptions in operations could cause increased supply costs or delays. Loss of significant supplier due to financial failure or other reasons may affect profitability. Dependence on global network of manufacturers and suppliers for product quality.

regulatory_risks: Environmental Protection Agency regulations on packaging, labeling, handling, transportation, storage and sale of chemicals and fertilizers
Occupational Safety and Health Administration requirements
Local fire codes governing storage of chemicals
Federal Insecticide, Fungicide and Rodenticide Act regulations for algaecides and pest control products
State pesticide laws requiring labeling, annual registration and licensing
Restrictions on ability to grant liens, incur indebtedness, make investments, merge or consolidate, and sell or transfer assets under credit facilities
Changing climate pattern regulations - new or increased legal and regulatory requirements to reduce or mitigate effects of climate change
Greenhouse gas emissions regulation potentially resulting in additional taxes or costs
European Union and state government data privacy regulations (California Consumer Privacy Rights Act)
Income tax law changes and accounting standards related to tax matters
Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules (Pillar Two) - minimum effective tax rate of 15% adopted by several countries
European and United States governmental bodies adopting laws requiring disclosure of emissions and similar data
Minimum wage or living wage requirements changes
Classification of exempt and non-exempt employees regulations
Other wage, labor or workplace regulations

sanction_risks: Major changes in tax policy, import or export regulations, or trade restrictions such as imposition of additional tariffs or duties on imported products could adversely affect business
Variability and complexity of tariffs and duties exposes to risk of higher costs and inadvertent noncompliance
United States has generally increased tariff rates and indicated additional increases could be forthcoming
Changes in laws, court rulings, or differences in interpretation on product classification could lead to changes in duty and tariff rates
